questions.question: What is the meaning of the term 'Environment'?
questions.correct_answer: Everything that we see in our surroundings
questions.explanation: It is our basic life support system, providing the air we breathe, the water we drink, and the land where we live.

questions.question: What are the three main components of the environment?
questions.explanation: Natural includes land and air; Human-made includes buildings and roads; Human includes individuals and society.

questions.question: Differentiate between Biotic and Abiotic environments.
questions.correct_answer: Biotic is the world of living organisms; Abiotic is the world of non-living elements
questions.explanation: Plants and animals are Biotic; Land and water are Abiotic.

questions.question: What is the Lithosphere?
questions.correct_answer: The solid crust or the hard top layer of the earth
questions.explanation: It is made up of rocks and minerals and covered by a thin layer of soil.

questions.question: Which domain of the environment is a source of mineral wealth?
questions.explanation: The lithosphere provides us with forests, grasslands, land for agriculture, and mineral deposits.

questions.question: Define the Hydrosphere.
questions.explanation: It comprises various sources of water like rivers, lakes, seas, and oceans.

questions.question: What holds the Atmosphere around the earth?
questions.correct_answer: The gravitational force of the earth
questions.explanation: The atmosphere is the thin layer of air that surrounds the earth and protects us from harmful rays.

questions.question: What is the Biosphere?
questions.correct_answer: A narrow zone of the earth where land, water, and air interact to support life
questions.explanation: Both plant and animal kingdom together make biosphere or the living world.

questions.question: What is an Ecosystem?
questions.correct_answer: The system formed by the interaction of all living organisms with each other and their physical environment
questions.explanation: All plants, animals, and human beings depend on their immediate surroundings and each other.

questions.question: Why do humans modify their environment?
questions.correct_answer: To fulfill their needs and adapt to their surroundings
questions.explanation: Humans have learned to grow crops, domesticate animals, and lead a settled life, creating things like industries and transport.

notes.title: Origin of Environment
notes.content: The word Environment comes from the French word 'Environer' meaning 'Neighborhood'.

notes.title: Atmosphere Function
notes.content: It protects us from the scorching heat and harmful UV rays of the sun.

notes.title: Human Environment
notes.content: Early humans adapted themselves to natural surroundings; modern humans modify it through technology.

facts.fact: World Environment Day is celebrated on 5th June every year.
Barter System is a trade in which goods are exchanged without the use of money.
The atmosphere consists of a number of gases, dust, and water vapour.
